feedback.text: I would kindly ask to consider adding protection against the destruction of video games to the Digital Fairness Act. At this point in time video games can be destroyed when a company doesn't want to support it anymore. This happened with multiple games I bought and liked playing (like The Crew and The Culling). Even though I bought them, I can never play them again because the companies behind them decided to destroy it after. This shouldn't be possible. When I buy a movie, song, book, etc. it becomes mine and I can enjoy it whenever I want. But when I buy a video game it can be taken away from me because the companies don't want me playing their old games, they want you to buy their new ones. I don't want to force the companies to work on their games forever or always keep their servers running, that's not at all what I'm asking for. I just want them to leave the game they sold me in a playable state when they decide not to support it anymore. I don't want to lose these pieces of art that many people worked their hardest for and that bring me so much joy, simply because of corporate greed.
